risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Randomization was in blocks of two stratified by infant gender." Comment: Although the exact sequence-generation algorithm is not named, blocked randomization with stratification indicates a systematic random allocation process rather than a predictable non-random rule; under the permissive criteria this is sufficient for Low risk.

Quote: "The allocation list was held by the pharmaceutical company and the Director of the Instituto de Investigacion Nutricional (IIN) until the end of the study." Comment: This indicates that the people enrolling participants did not have access to the allocation list, and the supplements were pre-coded with each subject’s unique study identification number with indistinguishable bottles, which is consistent with a mechanism preventing foreknowledge of the next assignment.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The bottles containing the supplements as well as the appearance of the supplements were indistinguishable." Comment: This indicates participants and personnel handling/distributing the supplements were likely blinded to allocation, and the intervention was a daily liquid supplement where knowing assignment could have influenced adherence and related outcomes; however, the indistinguishable bottles and coded allocation support successful blinding, so performance bias is likely low.

Quote: "Plasma ferritin was determined at the IIN laboratory by ELISA... Plasma copper and zinc and urinary zinc concentrations were determined at the IIN laboratory by atomic absorption spectrophotometry (AAS...)" and "The bottles containing the supplements as well as the appearance of the supplements were indistinguishable... The allocation list was held by the pharmaceutical company and the Director of the Instituto de Investigacion Nutricional (IIN) until the end of the study." Comment: The outcomes reported in this paper are biochemical laboratory measures (hemoglobin/hematocrit, ferritin, plasma copper, plasma zinc, urinary zinc), which are objective and measured by lab methods rather than subjective judgement. The trial also used indistinguishable bottles and concealed the allocation list, making knowledge of assignment unlikely for those processing outcome measurements. Because all reported outcomes in this study are objective and unlikely to be influenced by lack of blinding, detection bias is low.

Quote: "As shown, there were 42 (13%) infants who left the study, leaving 108 in the iron + copper group and 101 in the iron + copper + zinc group." and "those lost to follow up were significantly more likely to be girls and to be allocated to the supplemental zinc group. As shown in Figure 1, there were more children who left the study due to lack of time or because they disliked the supplement in the zinc group." Comment: Although overall loss to follow-up was 13%, attrition was clearly imbalanced between arms and the paper states more dropouts occurred in the zinc group, with reasons (disliked the supplement/lack of time) and sex imbalance suggesting differential missingness that could relate to outcome. This crosses the differential attrition threshold for High risk.
